questions.question: In the following question select the odd word from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: Wool is obtained from Sheep. Fur is obtained from cats/dogs/wof/fox etc Leather is obtained from rawhide of various animals like cow, buffalo, goat. Paper is made from plant extract which is the odd one.

questions.question: In the following question, select the related number from the given alternatives. 6827 : 120 ∷ 2945 : ?
In the following question, select the number which can be placed at the sign of question mark (?) from the given alternatives. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p5035/image003.jpg)

A word is represented by only one set of numbers as given in any one of the alternatives. The sets of numbers given in the alternatives are represented by two classes of alphabets as shown in the two matrices. The columns and rows of Matrix-I are numbered from 0 to 4 and that of Matrix-II are numbered from 5 to 9. A letter from these matrices can be represented by its row and next by its column, for example, 'J' can be represented by 10, and 'Q' can be represented by 30, 88. Similarly, you have to identify the set for the word "EARS". Matrix - I: 01234 0CWRLO 1JHYVI 2NEZMP 3QKDFB 4GWTSA Matrix - II: 56789 5ZGVPB 6KIAMT 7RSWOF 8UNDQC 9HEJLX
Arrange the given words in the sequence in which they occur in the dictionary. 1. Juncture 2. Jubilant 3. Junketer 4. Jeweller 5. Jigglier 6. Jaeger
questions.explanation: I=$ �ULQX!EXBAg<I=$ �ULQX!EXBAg<

questions.question: In the following question, select the odd number pair from the given alternatives.
questions.explanation: The sum of the squares is taken 9+4+1= 14 4+16+25= 45 1+9+36= 46 and not 48 1+4+16= 21

questions.question: R is related to SQ in the same way as J is related to?

Six persons are sitting in a linear row facing south. C is sitting second to the right of H. L is at the corner. S is sitting to the immediate left of Z. Y is sitting second to the right of Z. H is to the right of L. Who is sitting to the immediate right of H?
questions.explanation: L is at the corner. H is to the right of L. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6065/image032.jpg) S is sitting to the immediate left of Z. Y is sitting second to the right of Z. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6065/image033.jpg) C is sitting second to the right of H. So, case 2 is invalid. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p6065/image034.png) Hence, Y is sitting to the immediate right of H.

questions.question: In the following question, from the given alternative words, select the word which cannot be formed using the letters of the given word. MYSTERIOUS
questions.explanation: OYESTER cannot be formed.

questions.question: In a certain code language 'GROUND' is coded as 'TSLVME'. How is 'AUGUST' coded in that language?
questions.explanation: The letters alternatively are changed to reversed alphabet and next letter. A → Z U+1=V G → T U+1=V S → H T+1=U Code for AUGUST is ZVTVHU

questions.question: If P is coded as '+', Q is coded as '-', R is coded as ' X ' and S is coded as '÷', then what is the value of 63S7P28Q17R2?

Arrange the following words in a meaningful logical sequence and choose the appropriate number sequence from the alternatives. 1. Admission in school 2. Passing the test 3. Filling the application form 4. Going to school
questions.explanation: Applying → passing the test → admission → going to school

questions.question: Which of the following figures among I, II, III and IV depict the correct cube obtained on folding the given unfolded hollow cube (X)? ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p7040/image005.jpg)
questions.explanation: On folding the given unfolded cube, we get cube with pattern as in II.

questions.question: In a certain coded language RAT= 38 and SAD= 23 then which of the following will be the code for BAD?
questions.explanation: If we assign position values to alphabets such that A= 1, B=2, C= 3 and so on, then to get the code, the position value of 3rd alphabet is added to the product of the 1st 2 position values R= 18, A=1, T=20; 18x1+20= 38 S=19, A=1, D=4; 19x1+4= 23 B=2, A=1, D=4; 2x1+4= 6

questions.question: M has to walk towards his east in order to reach A. A walks towards south-west to reach E. In between the path from where A went to E, N starts walking towards south in order to reach K. E has to walk towards his east in order to reach H. Suppose, N meets EH at point O during their way to their destinations, what will be the position of N and E's meeting point with respect to the M's starting point?

questions.explanation: Emerald is green and ruby is red in colour.

questions.question: If P $ Q means P is father of Q, P # Q means P is mother of Q, P * Q means P is sister of Q, then how is Q related to N in N # L $ P * Q?
questions.explanation: Solution: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/clerkimg/3313/image012.jpg) Here we don't know whether Q is male or female. Hence we can't determine how Q is related to N.

questions.question: Which of the following options will complete the given series: _ A _ _ C _ B _ _ A _ B
questions.explanation: The series is: CABB CABB CABB

questions.question: In each of the questions below are given some statements followed by two conclusions numbered I and II. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance from commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts. Statements: Some drills are drugs. No drug is a dam. No drill is a laker. Conclusions: I. Some lakers are drugs. II. Some dams are drills.
options: If either Conclusion I or II follows.
If neither Conclusion I nor II follows.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/syllogism/image0018809_drills_drugs.jpg) Neither conclusion follows
